event_blocks.summary: Inside a service van, a worker wearing black gloves retrieves tools from a black tool bag, preparing for a service call to repair an "old unit" possibly from the 70s. The scene shifts to a rooftop environment with solar panels and suburban houses, where the worker identifies the HVAC unit and begins disassembly using a red and black cordless drill to remove screws from the beige metal housing panels. Sounds of drilling, metal scraping, and clanking accompany the removal of panels, revealing internal wiring. The worker confirms the unit is an R22 and later identifies components as suction, high pressure, and furnace assembly. After successfully lighting the pilot, indicated by a steady hiss, the worker tests electrical connections with a yellow multimeter, noting the blower motor starts and then turns off. The worker expresses satisfaction at lighting the unit on the first try, contrasting with a previous technician's inability to do so, and continues inspecting internal components with the multimeter while the drill is placed nearby on a brown metal surface.

Black Tool Bag.description: A black, zippered bag with internal gray compartments, labeled with stickers including 'HVAC' and 'YETI PRO PAC'. The audio includes sounds of tools being handled and batteries being 'popped in' as the technician retrieves items from it.

Black Gloves.description: Worn by the technician, providing grip and protection while handling tools and removing screws from the metal housing; visible throughout the segment as the technician manipulates the drill and panels.

Solar Panels.description: Large black rectangular panels installed on the roof, visible in the background, indicating a renewable energy setup; remain static throughout the segment.

Milwaukee PACKOUT Toolboxes.description: Stacked red toolboxes with black accents, labeled 'Milwaukee PACKOUT', some featuring stickers like 'YETI' and 'HVAC'. The audio includes sounds of tools being handled, implying interaction with the tools stored within or near these boxes inside the service van.

Rooftop Environment.description: A gray shingled roof with solar panels, vents, and a chimney, surrounded by suburban houses and greenery under clear, sunny skies; serves as the backdrop for the maintenance work.

Cordless Drill.description: A red and black power tool, labeled 'FUEL BRUSHLESS' and 'M18 REDLITHIUM XC', actively used to unscrew fasteners from the beige metal housing. Its operation is clearly audible with drilling and unscrewing sounds, and the technician explicitly says "Screw" while using it.

Brown Metal Surface.description: A flat, rust-colored metal structure on the roof, possibly a vent or equipment housing, where tools are placed and from which panels are removed.

Metal Housing Panels.description: Beige, rectangular metal panels forming the sides of the HVAC unit, being unscrewed and removed by the technician to access internal wiring and components; show signs of wear and dirt.

Internal Wiring.description: A bundle of white and colored wires visible inside the opened metal housing, suggesting electrical or HVAC system components; exposed as panels are removed.

Beige Metal Housing.description: A large, curved, beige metal structure on the roof, housing the HVAC unit. The technician's dialogue confirms this is a 'furnace' that needs to be 'lit' and its temperature checked, directly linking the visual housing to the task described verbally, and sounds of work being done on it are heard.

Yellow Multimeter.description: A handheld yellow device with red and black probes, used to test electrical connections inside the HVAC unit; held by the technician’s gloved hand during inspection.

Service Van Interior.description: The interior of a white and blue service van, featuring black flooring, white shelving units, and organized storage for tools and equipment. The audio features sounds of tools being handled and batteries being inserted, occurring within this setting as the technician retrieves equipment.

raw_segments: The video opens inside a well-organized white work van, showcasing neatly arranged Milwaukee PACKOUT toolboxes, some adorned with 'YETI' and 'HVAC' stickers. A person wearing black gloves opens a black tool bag, revealing its interior compartments, as a male voice (Speaker 1) announces they are on a service call and needs to "put these in my bag" and "bring out the probes." A conversation unfolds between Speaker 1 and two others (Speaker 2, male; Speaker 3, female) about an "old unit," possibly from the 70s, with Speaker 1 offering to "take a look right now" to repair it, despite Speaker 3's skepticism. The person retrieves a small black tool from the bag. Speaker 1 concludes the interaction, stating they will "take a look at what we can do." The scene then transitions to a bright, daylight rooftop setting, covered with gray shingles and featuring solar panels, vents, and a chimney, surrounded by suburban houses. Speaker 1 declares, "This is the unit," and expresses intent to "take a look at the data plate," accompanied by a metal clanking sound. The person places the small black tool on a brown metal surface near the solar panels, their shadow cast prominently on the roof. Speaker 1 mentions the "M12 drill" and a co-worker, adding that he has to use "the big boy." The person then picks up a red and black cordless drill and places it beside the small tool, preparing for work, while Speaker 1 utters "Fraser Johnston, Johnston," possibly reading a name.

The video opens with a first-person perspective of a worker on a gray shingled rooftop, surrounded by suburban houses and greenery under bright sunlight, with solar panels visible. The worker, wearing black gloves and dark work pants, immediately states, "No data plate that I can read," then identifies the equipment as, "This is going to be an R22 unit." After a brief "Um," a sound of something being placed is heard. The worker then says, "First, easier to go in my bag now. Gotta find some room for it," as he approaches a brown metal surface where a red and black cordless drill and a black tool bag, labeled 'HVAC' and 'YETI PRO PAC', are placed. He continues, "Assuming we're gonna be right here," before using the cordless drill to remove screws from the metal housing, accompanied by the distinct sound of the drill operating. A grunt is heard as he continues, stating, "take this one out too," followed by another drill sound. He explains his reasoning, "I'm assuming it's right here because of the box shooting this way," then corrects himself, "see if I'm right. Oh wait, no, the compressor's right here." The worker decides, "We'll just open it up," anticipating, "We'll probably have to get through here to put my gauges, my probes." As he lifts a side panel of the rusted metal housing, revealing internal wiring, sounds of metal scraping and clanking are heard, and he concludes with a "Yeah."

Under bright, sunny conditions, a technician, seen from a first-person perspective and wearing black gloves, works on a rooftop HVAC unit. The worker's voice is heard confirming his assessment, stating, "Yeah, I think I was right. Yeah, okay." He then identifies internal components, remarking, "This is gonna be my suction. This is gonna be my high pressure. This might be the furnace assembly." Throughout this, he actively uses a red and black cordless drill, labeled 'FUEL BRUSHLESS' and 'M18 REDLITHIUM XC', to remove screws from the beige metal housing panels. The distinct whirring and drilling sounds of the tool are prominent as fasteners are unscrewed. As panels are loosened, the worker manually pulls them away, revealing internal wiring and components, accompanied by metallic clanking and scraping sounds, and a distinct clang/thud as a larger panel is manipulated. The surrounding gray shingled roof features solar panels, vents, and a chimney, with suburban houses and greenery visible in the background, all while the worker's shadow subtly shifts, indicating the passage of time during this deliberate disassembly for maintenance or repair.

The minute opens with the distinct sound of a cordless drill operating as a worker, wearing black gloves, meticulously removes screws from the beige metal housing of an HVAC unit on a gray shingled rooftop. After the drill stops, clanking metal sounds accompany the removal of panels, exposing internal wiring. The camera pans to reveal the sunny suburban environment with neighboring houses and solar panels. The worker's clear male voice is heard saying, "Wow, look at that," as he identifies the unit as a "furnace assembly." He then explains his diagnostic process, stating, "Alright, so I'm having her turn on heat from the thermostat. Let's see what goes on," and later, "Get a call for heat." As he tests electrical connections with a yellow multimeter, the distinct whirring sound of a blower motor starts, which the worker confirms, "Okay, blower motor's on." The whirring continues before he notes, "Blower motor turned off." He then points out, "There's the pilot," and attempts to light it, with clicking ignition sounds followed by a steady hiss, indicating a successful ignition and gas flow. The worker observes, "I see the thermal coupling," as the gas continues to hiss, emphasizing the precision of his work on the internal components of the beige metal housing.

The video opens with a first-person perspective of a technician, wearing black gloves, standing on a gray shingled rooftop under bright sunlight. The technician's voice is immediately heard confirming, "Okay, lit," followed by an explanation of the task: "Furnace is lit. So what we're gonna do is check the temperature that's coming out. I should actually shut that door up there first. Let me do that." He begins working on a large beige metal housing, part of an HVAC unit, using a red and black cordless drill labeled 'FUEL BRUSHLESS' to remove screws from its panels, revealing internal wiring amidst the distinct sounds of drilling and mechanical clanking. After placing the drill on the roof, the scene briefly transitions to the well-organized interior of a service van, where the technician retrieves tools from a black tool bag labeled 'HVAC' and 'YETI PRO PAC', and organizes them near stacked red Milwaukee PACKOUT toolboxes. This is accompanied by sounds of tools being handled and batteries being "popped in." A distinct bang or thud is heard, possibly a door closing. The technician then returns to the rooftop, continuing work on the HVAC unit. He expresses satisfaction, stating, "She said the last person couldn't get it lit. Interesting. Got it lit first try. It's lit. I like that." He then resumes using the drill, with more drilling sounds and the technician saying "Screw," as he continues to inspect the internal components with a yellow multimeter. The surrounding environment includes solar panels, a chimney, and neighboring suburban houses, all under clear skies, providing a backdrop to the technician’s deliberate and focused maintenance task.

event_blocks.summary: On a sunny rooftop, a technician wearing black gloves uses a cordless drill to remove screws from a beige metal housing, the whirring and clicking of the tool audible against a backdrop of gray shingled roofing, solar panels, and suburban houses. After placing the drill on a brown metal surface, the scene shifts indoors to a dimly lit living room where the technician, still gloved, states, "All right, what are we pushing? Pushing 106 degrees. It's climbing still," while adjusting a Honeywell thermostat. A small white dog walks by, and the technician commands, "Hey puppy. Sit," followed by panting sounds. A digital thermometer hanging from the ceiling displays '120 degrees', with on-screen text confirming 'REACHED 120 DEGREES'. The technician then adjusts a ceiling fan, saying, "Turn off, turn cool," accompanied by the sound of the fan being manipulated. The camera pans across the living room, revealing a wooden entertainment center, a couch, and a doorway to a kitchen where the dog runs through. A female voice inquires about the rooftop work, "Are they servicing the unit up there or replacing it?" to which the technician replies, "They're servicing it... Yeah, they're um, doing a cleaning from what it looks like." The conversation continues with the female voice noting, "They're doing a lot of stuff with that house too," and the technician observing, "I saw that, yeah. Demolishing some walls or something and... I wonder if they're gonna try to sell it or something."

Honeywell Thermostat.description: A white digital thermostat mounted on a textured wall, displaying a green screen; adjusted by the technician’s gloved hand to change the temperature setting, with the technician verbally noting the temperature is "pushing 106 degrees" and "climbing still."

Ceiling Fan.description: A white ceiling fan with a circular design, located in the living room; adjusted by the technician’s hands, with the technician verbally instructing to "Turn off, turn cool," and accompanied by adjustment sounds, while a digital thermometer hangs from it.

Digital Thermometer.description: A black digital device hanging from the ceiling fan, displaying '120 degrees' in white text; used to monitor indoor temperature, which the technician notes is "climbing still."

Living Room Interior.description: A dimly lit room with tiled floors, beige walls, a wooden entertainment center, a couch, and a ceiling fan; features a doorway leading to a kitchen with visible trash can and appliances; serves as the setting for the technician's indoor work and conversations with an unseen female.

Solar Panels.description: Large black rectangular panels installed on the roof, visible in the background, indicating a renewable energy setup; remain static throughout the segment.

Small White Dog.description: A fluffy, light-colored dog seen walking on the tiled floor indoors, moving near the technician’s feet and running through the kitchen area, to whom the technician speaks, saying "Hey puppy. Sit," and whose panting can be heard.

Kitchen Area.description: A bright, tiled space visible through a doorway, containing a trash can, countertop, and various household items; the small white dog runs through this area.

Rooftop Environment.description: A gray shingled roof with solar panels, vents, and a chimney, surrounded by suburban houses and greenery under clear, sunny skies; serves as the backdrop for the HVAC servicing, which is discussed in dialogue.

Yellow Multimeter.description: A handheld yellow device with red and black probes, used to test electrical connections inside the HVAC unit; held by the technician’s gloved hand during inspection.

Cordless Drill.description: A red and black power tool labeled 'FUEL BRUSHLESS' and 'M18 REDLITHIUM XC', used to unscrew fasteners from the beige metal housing on the rooftop, accompanied by distinct whirring and clicking sounds; later placed on a brown metal surface.

event_blocks.summary: Transitioning from a dimly lit indoor living space to a bright, sunny rooftop environment, a technician wearing Black Gloves works on a Beige Metal Housing unit amidst Solar Panels. A male voice explains the necessity of tuning up the air conditioning unit before a sale, noting previous failed attempts to 'light it' and the decision to replace the 'old' unit. He clarifies that insurance complications prevent immediate replacement, as they require the unit to be 'not working' and prefer repair to get it 'up and running.' The technician opens the Beige Metal Housing, exposing Internal Wiring and a cylindrical component, then retrieves pliers and a screwdriver from a Black Tool Bag. He meticulously manipulates the color-coded wiring at a terminal block while the male voice announces, 'We're going to check the refrigeration levels.' As the technician uses pliers to adjust wires and then employs a Yellow Multimeter to test connections, a consistent humming or whirring sound is audible. The camera focuses on the technician’s gloved hands against the backdrop of the Rooftop Environment, highlighting the detailed inspection of the HVAC unit's internal components.

Black Tool Bag.description: A black, zippered bag with internal gray compartments, opened to reveal tools; labeled with stickers including 'HVAC' and 'YETI PRO PAC', used to store and access tools during the task.

Black Gloves.description: Worn by the technician throughout, providing grip and protection while handling tools and adjusting the thermostat; visible during both rooftop and indoor tasks.

Solar Panels.description: Large black rectangular panels installed on the roof, visible in the background, indicating a renewable energy setup; remain static throughout the segment.

Beige Metal Housing.description: A large, curved, beige metal structure on the roof, identified as an 'AC' unit that is 'old' and needs to be 'tuned up' and 'up and running'. Its internal components are being accessed for repair, with its 'refrigeration levels' needing to be checked, and its status is central to discussions about insurance-covered replacement versus repair.

Rooftop Environment.description: A gray shingled roof with solar panels, vents, and a chimney, surrounded by suburban houses and greenery under clear, sunny skies; serves as the backdrop for the maintenance work.

Yellow Multimeter.description: A handheld yellow device with red and black probes, used to test electrical connections inside the HVAC unit; held by the technician’s gloved hand during inspection.

Metal Housing Panels.description: Beige, rectangular metal panels forming the sides of the HVAC unit, being unscrewed and removed by the technician to access internal wiring and components; show signs of wear and dirt.

Internal Wiring.description: A bundle of white and colored wires visible inside the opened metal housing, suggesting electrical or HVAC system components; exposed as panels are removed.

raw_segments: The video opens indoors in a dimly lit living room, briefly showing a doorway leading to a kitchen and a window with blinds, before quickly transitioning to a bright, sunny rooftop. Here, a technician, wearing Black Gloves, is actively working on a Beige Metal Housing unit mounted on a gray shingled roof, surrounded by Solar Panels and suburban greenery. A male voice explains that they need to "get the AC all tuned up before they sell it," noting that the unit needs to be "up and running." He elaborates that previous attempts to "light it" failed, leading to a decision to replace the "old" unit. A brief, interrupted female voice is heard before the male voice clarifies that the replacement is complicated by insurance, which "won't replace units unless they're not working," preferring repair to get the unit "up and running." The technician proceeds to open the Beige Metal Housing, revealing Internal Wiring and a large cylindrical component. He retrieves pliers and a screwdriver from a Black Tool Bag placed atop the unit, then meticulously manipulates the wiring at a color-coded terminal block. The male voice states, "We're going to check the refrigeration levels." As the technician uses pliers to adjust wires and then picks up a Yellow Multimeter to test connections, a consistent humming or whirring sound emanates, likely from the HVAC unit or a tool. The camera maintains a close focus on the technician’s gloved hands, with the Rooftop Environment of neighboring houses and trees serving as a clear backdrop, emphasizing the technical repair or inspection of the HVAC unit's internal components.

event_blocks.summary: The technician, wearing black gloves, begins work on a beige metal HVAC unit on a rooftop under a clear sky, amidst gray shingles and solar panels. A constant whirring sound accompanies his actions as he manipulates internal wiring, uses a smartphone for inspection, and employs a yellow multimeter and cordless drill visible on a brown metal surface. He reports diagnostic readings, stating "All right, 183 PSI, the high side, 50 PSI on the low side. That's low-ish. Should be at 70 or 80," and later "Alright, we're reading 56.4 PSI on the suction" and "220 PSI on the high side," concluding "This is a little low on refrigeration." He explains to a female voice, "All right, so I plugged in my probes to check the refrigeration," discussing the unit's performance and R22 refrigerant, noting "I took the filter out to see if it made any change," but "not much," confirming it's "still a little low on refrigeration." The scene transitions indoors to a dimly lit living room with beige walls, where a small white dog runs through an adjacent kitchen area. A ceiling fan, from which a black digital thermometer hangs displaying '120 degrees' (confirmed by on-screen text 'REACHED 120 DEGREES'), is adjusted by the technician, who also interacts with a Honeywell thermostat. Another male voice discusses "50-degree temperature" and "60 right now," explaining temperature dependency on "refrigeration that you have up there" and potential leaks, while the first technician elaborates on cleaning the condenser, checking pressures, and refrigerant levels, noting if it's "low."

raw_segments: The minute opens on a rooftop with a close-up of a technician's gloved hands, wearing black gloves, working inside an open, worn beige metal HVAC unit. A constant whirring sound accompanies the scene as the technician manipulates internal wiring and tests connections with a handheld device, revealing labeled terminals. He states, "All right, 183 PSI, the high side, 50 PSI on the low side. That's low-ish. Should be at 70 or 80," indicating a diagnostic check. The rooftop environment, with its gray shingles and distant solar panels under a clear sky, provides the backdrop. The scene then transitions indoors to a dimly lit living room, where the technician continues his work, adjusting a white ceiling fan from which a black digital thermometer hangs, prominently displaying '120 degrees'. The whirring sound persists. As the camera pans, a well-furnished interior with a wooden entertainment center, bookshelves, and a couch is revealed, along with a small white dog running through the adjacent kitchen area. The technician explains to a female voice, "All right, so I plugged in my probes to check the refrigeration," and discusses the unit's performance and the R22 refrigerant, which the female voice acknowledges with "Okay" and "For various reasons." On-screen text confirms the indoor temperature, stating 'REACHED 120 DEGREES', emphasizing the shift from outdoor maintenance to indoor verification.

The video opens indoors in a dimly lit living room with beige walls, where a small white dog runs through an adjacent kitchen area. A ceiling fan, from which a digital thermometer hangs displaying '120 degrees' (confirmed by on-screen text 'REACHED 120 DEGREES'), is adjusted by a technician wearing black gloves. The technician also interacts with a Honeywell thermostat on the wall. A male voice (Technician 1) is heard discussing a "50-degree temperature" and "60 right now," explaining that temperature depends on the "refrigeration that you have up there," and mentioning issues like leaks. Another male voice (Technician 2) interjects about "two nubs," prompting Technician 1 to elaborate on cleaning the condenser, checking pressures, and refrigerant levels, noting if it's "low." The scene then transitions to a bright, sunny rooftop environment featuring gray shingles and solar panels. The technician, still in black gloves, methodically works on a beige metal HVAC housing unit, which emits a consistent hum and whirring sound. He uses a smartphone to record or inspect the internal wiring, which includes a bundle of white and colored wires connected to a terminal block. A cordless drill and a yellow multimeter are visible nearby on a brown metal surface. Technician 1 provides live diagnostic readings, stating, "Alright, we're reading 56.4 PSI on the suction" and "220 PSI on the high side," concluding that "This is a little low on refrigeration." He further explains, "I took the filter out to see if it made any change," but notes "not much," confirming it's "still a little low on refrigeration" before stating, "Pretty much done," as the HVAC unit continues its operational hum.

event_blocks.summary: A technician, wearing black gloves, works on a rooftop HVAC unit, using a red and black cordless drill to remove screws from a beige metal housing. The whirring of the drill is audible as he exposes internal wiring and tests connections with a yellow multimeter, documenting the process with a smartphone displaying a camera interface. The scene shifts to the interior of a service van, where the technician, now in a black t-shirt, explains a 'no heating' call, his success in lighting the furnace where others failed, and advises a new system due to the unit's poor condition, R22 refrigerant phase-out, and low refrigerant levels. He notes the unit wouldn't keep up in summer despite current cooling. The camera then shows a GMC van's interior from the driver's seat, with the technician's hand on the steering wheel, a navigation map on the dashboard smartphone, and a music app on another smartphone. The van drives down a suburban street, accompanied by hip-hop music, en route to the next job.

Technician.description: A man with dark curly hair and a mustache, wearing a black t-shirt, seated in the service van, speaking directly to the camera about a 'no heating' call, a quote for a new system, the phase-out of R22 refrigerant, and the unit's low refrigerant and inability to cool in summer; later seen driving the GMC van with his hand on the steering wheel, wearing a black watch.

Smartphone (Left Side Mount).description: A black smartphone mounted on the left side of the dashboard, displaying a music app with a pink fox image, indicating the technician’s personal media use, playing background hip-hop/rap music.

Internal Wiring.description: A bundle of white and colored wires visible inside the opened metal housing, suggesting electrical or HVAC system components; exposed as panels are removed.

raw_segments: The minute opens with a first-person perspective of a technician, clad in black gloves, dark pants, and black shoes, diligently working on a rooftop HVAC unit. The distinct whirring sound of a cordless drill, a red and black 'FUEL BRUSHLESS M18 REDLITHIUM XC' model, accompanies his actions as he removes screws from a beige metal housing on the gray shingled roof, surrounded by solar panels and suburban greenery under a clear sky. After exposing the internal wiring, he uses a yellow multimeter to test connections, while holding a black smartphone, its screen displaying a camera interface with a red record button, to document the process. The scene then transitions to the bright, clean interior of a white and blue service van, where the technician, now seated and wearing a black t-shirt, speaks directly to the camera. Behind him, neatly organized red Milwaukee PACKOUT toolboxes, some adorned with 'YETI' and 'HVAC' stickers, are visible alongside a blue air compressor. The technician explains that he gave a quote for a new system after a 'no heating' call, noting that previous technicians couldn't get the furnace to light, but he succeeded on the first try, suggesting they might have been trying to upsell. He further elaborates on the unit's poor condition, the phase-out of R22 refrigerant, and why a new unit is preferable to repairs, mentioning the unit was low on refrigerant and wouldn't keep up in summer despite currently cooling. As he finishes speaking, the camera shifts to a forward-facing view from the driver’s seat of the GMC van. His hand, wearing a black watch, grips the steering wheel while a black smartphone on the dashboard displays a navigation map and another on the left shows a music app with a pink fox image. The van drives down a suburban street, passing houses and trees under a partly cloudy sky, accompanied by the rhythmic beat and male vocals of a hip-hop/rap track, signaling his journey to the next job.

event_blocks.summary: From the first-person perspective within a GMC van, two smartphones are visible on the dashboard, one displaying a music app with a pink fox and the other a navigation map, as the vehicle travels down a suburban street. The scene shifts to a technician, clad in black gloves, kneeling by the open rear of the van amidst the sounds of rummaging and clanking from Milwaukee PACKOUT toolboxes. The technician announces, "All right, we need a transformer," and specifies "115" while retrieving and examining a small white 'Packard' box. Subsequently, the technician is shown working on the internal wiring of an HVAC unit on a rooftop, explaining that the customer's transformer had failed after a previous self-repair attempt, leading to the discovery of a burnt spot on the motherboard where the door switch was connected, with no solder remaining. After replacing the motherboard and a new transformer, the system functioned correctly without further shorting. The technician uses a yellow multimeter to test connections, seeking shorts, and confirms, "But now the system's up and running," noting, "It won't be as bright, it won't be that bright," as the HVAC unit's hum and whirring become audible.

Milwaukee PACKOUT Toolboxes.description: Stacked red toolboxes with black accents, labeled 'Milwaukee PACKOUT', some featuring stickers like 'YETI' and 'HVAC', stored inside the service van, from which sounds of rummaging and clanking are heard as the technician accesses them, stating he needs a 'transformer' and mentioning '115'.

Packard Box.description: A small white box labeled 'Packard' with blue and black text, retrieved from the Milwaukee PACKOUT toolbox and inspected by the technician while he is heard stating he needs a 'transformer'.

Smartphone (Dashboard Mount).description: A black smartphone mounted on the dashboard, displaying a navigation map with a blue route and location pin, used for route guidance during the drive.

Black Gloves.description: Worn by the technician during rooftop work, providing grip and protection while handling tools and inspecting the HVAC unit’s internal wiring, as he explains the repair process.

Green Circuit Board.description: A teal-colored printed circuit board with visible solder points, traces, and mounting holes, held by the technician’s gloved hand during inspection of the HVAC unit’s internal components, which he identifies as the 'motherboard' that had a 'burnt spot' and was subsequently 'replaced'.

Yellow Multimeter.description: A handheld yellow device with red and black probes, used by the technician’s gloved hand to test electrical connections inside the HVAC unit, as he mentions 'trying to see if there was any shorts'.

Service Van Interior.description: The interior of a white and blue service van, featuring black flooring, white shelving units, and organized storage for tools and equipment; includes a blue air compressor and various toolboxes, with the van’s side door open to reveal the technician’s workspace, where sounds of rummaging are heard.

GMC Van Steering Wheel.description: A black steering wheel with the GMC logo, gripped by the technician’s hand as he drives the van down a suburban street.

Technician.description: A man with dark curly hair and a mustache, wearing a black t-shirt, initially seen driving the service van, then kneeling at the back of the van, and later working on an HVAC unit on a rooftop. He speaks clearly and informatively, explaining the service call, the issue with a 'burnt spot' on the 'motherboard' and a faulty 'transformer', the repair process of replacing the board, and confirming the system is now 'up and running' with the sound of the HVAC unit operating.

Smartphone (Left Side Mount).description: A black smartphone mounted on the left side of the dashboard, displaying a music app with a pink fox image, indicating the technician’s personal media use.

Internal Wiring.description: A bundle of white and colored wires visible inside the opened metal housing of the HVAC unit, which the technician works on while explaining the electrical issues and repairs, including a 'burnt spot' and 'no solder left'.

raw_segments: The video opens with a first-person view from inside a service van, showing the dashboard with two smartphones mounted, one displaying a music app with a pink fox image and the other a navigation map, as the van drives down a suburban street. The scene transitions to the technician, wearing black gloves, kneeling beside the open back of the van, where sounds of rummaging and clanking are heard from the organized Milwaukee PACKOUT toolboxes. The technician's voice is heard stating, "All right, we need a transformer," and mentioning "115" as he retrieves a small white 'Packard' box and inspects it. The camera then shifts to the interior of an HVAC unit on a rooftop, where the technician is working on the internal wiring. He explains the service call, stating, "All right, for this service call, the customer's transformer went out. He went ahead and made the repair himself. It kept going out. So I found that the motherboard had a burnt spot behind it." He continues, detailing that "That burnt spot was where the door switch was connected to. There was no solder left. Um so that was his issue." As he manipulates the wiring and handles a green circuit board, he explains, "We replaced the board. As soon as we replaced the board and put a new transformer, everything was great and uh nothing shorted out and caused the transformer to burn out again." He uses a yellow multimeter to test connections, adding, "Here I was trying to see if there was any shorts." The technician concludes, "But now the system's up and running," and as the distinct hum and whirring of the HVAC unit operating are heard, he remarks, "It won't be as bright, it won't be that bright."

event_blocks.summary: Inside a well-lit service van interior, a technician begins unboxing a package from DiversiTech, which owns Hilmor, announcing the arrival of new equipment. With a background hum and rustling sounds, he methodically sorts through a brown cardboard box, identifying various packaged tools including a 2-inch hole cutter, an inspection mirror, a compact Micron gauge, a valve core removal tool with a magnet, cutters, a hand seamer, a duct knife, a zip tie tensioning tool, a stubby screwdriver, crimpers, an all-in-one screwdriver, nut drivers, a utility knife, wire strippers, pocket breaks, and a hose for a vacuum pump, noting the Micron gauge is 'nice' and mentioning 'Seal-Lok'. He also pulls out a blue and white box for a 'FRESH AIR UV MINI UV LED' light, describing it as a UV light for mini splits that improves indoor air quality. The technician expresses his intention to stock his van with these new Hilmar Tools, including green and black screwdrivers, pliers, a digital level, and a metal tool, organizing them into his 'install bag,' a black 'VETO PRO PAC' tool bag. He notes he is replacing an old hand seamer and zip tie tensioning tool with new ones, and plans to make a list for future additions to complete the bag. Elsewhere, a red 'weather guard' metal tray mounted on the exterior of the white service van holds two red '5X COIL CLEANER' spray cans, and a bright red plastic tray is removed from cardboard boxes on a wooden pallet. Stacked red Milwaukee PACKOUT toolboxes and a black tool bag labeled 'HVAC' are visible in the organized van interior.

Black Tool Bag.description: A black, zippered bag labeled 'VETO PRO PAC', shown with an organized interior containing green-handled screwdrivers, a black flashlight, a white container with red wires, and explicitly mentioned by a male voice to hold 'blades and a charger'.

Breez Edge Pro Smart Thermostat.description: A white rectangular box with blue and black text, labeled 'Breez Edge Pro Smart Thermostat for Mini-Split', held up by the technician for inspection.

Milwaukee PACKOUT Toolboxes.description: Stacked red toolboxes with black accents, labeled 'Milwaukee PACKOUT', some featuring stickers like 'YETI' and 'HVAC', stored inside the service van and used for organizing tools.

Mini UV LED Light.description: A black and green handheld device in clear packaging, labeled 'Mini UV LED', retrieved from the box and briefly displayed, which the technician describes as "the UV for mini splits" that "improves indoor quality" and has a "UV blue light."

Hilmar Tools.description: Various tools in clear plastic packaging with green and black branding, including screwdrivers, pliers, a digital level, and a metal tool, which the technician explicitly identifies as "some of the Hilmar tools that we'll be adding to my install bag," and also mentions a "new hand seamer" and a "new zip tie tensioning tool" being added or replaced.

Metal Tool (Hilmar).description: A long, silver metal tool in clear packaging with green and black Hilmar branding, pulled from the box and shown to the camera, which the technician refers to as a "new hand seamer" and a "new zip tie tensioning tool" that are being replaced or added to the bag.

Service Van Interior.description: The interior of a white and blue service van, featuring black flooring, white shelving units, and organized storage for tools and equipment; includes a blue air compressor and various toolboxes, with the van’s side door open to reveal the technician’s workspace, which the technician states they are "stocking up" with new tools.

Cardboard Box.description: A brown cardboard box filled with new, packaged tools and equipment, placed on the floor of the service van, being sorted through by the technician’s hand, from which the technician retrieves various items while narrating.

Technician.description: A man with dark curly hair and a mustache, wearing a black t-shirt, seated in the service van, whose clear voice is heard throughout the segment as he unboxes and describes various tools, identifying the package source as 'DiversiTech' and mentioning 'Hilmor' as a brand they own.

Digital Level.description: A compact digital level in clear packaging with green and black branding, held up by the technician for inspection, and identified as one of the Hilmar tools being added to the install bag.

Cardboard Boxes.description: Brown cardboard boxes labeled 'weather guard', stacked on a wooden pallet, containing red plastic trays; used for packaging and transporting the trays.

White Service Van.description: A white van with black textured side panels, serving as the backdrop for organizing supplies, which is explicitly mentioned by a male voice as the subject of an upcoming 'van tour'.

Red Plastic Tray.description: A bright red plastic tray with molded edges and internal ridges, removed from a cardboard box; designed to fit into the packaging and likely used for organizing or transporting tools or supplies.

Wooden Pallet.description: A wooden pallet with slats, supporting stacked cardboard boxes; used as a temporary work surface for unpacking and handling the red plastic trays.

Red Metal Tray.description: A bright red metal tray labeled 'weather guard', mounted on the side of a white van, which a male voice identifies as 'this little thing' he installed, and describes as 'perfect' for holding cans, accompanied by metallic clanking sounds as items are placed within its multiple holes and slots.

Red Spray Cans.description: Two red cylindrical cans labeled '5X COIL CLEANER' with a cartoon character logo, which a male voice refers to as 'cans like this' while metallic clanking sounds are heard as they are placed into the red metal tray.

raw_segments: The video transitions from a rooftop HVAC maintenance scene to the interior of a well-lit service van, where a technician, whose clear voice is heard, begins unboxing new equipment. With a background hum present, he announces, "Alright guys, DiversiTech sent us a package. DiversiTech owns Hilmor, and they own a lot of other brands. Let's see what we got." His hand, wearing a black wristband, methodically sorts through a brown cardboard box, pulling out various packaged tools. Rustling sounds accompany the unboxing as he identifies items like a "2-inch 20-inch hole cutter for sheet metal plenums," an "inspection mirror," and a "compact Micron gauge," which he notes is "nice." He continues to describe a "valve core removal tool with a magnet on top" for condensing units, "cutters or dikes," a "hand seamer," a "duct knife," a "zip tie tensioning tool," a "stubby screwdriver," "crimpers," an "all-in-one screwdriver," "nut drivers," a "utility knife," "wire strippers," "pocket breaks," and a "hose for my vacuum pump," before mentioning "their Seal-Lok" as the minute concludes. Throughout, stacked red Milwaukee PACKOUT toolboxes and a black tool bag labeled 'HVAC' are visible in the organized van interior, providing context for the preparation of new tools, some of which include a Hilmar brand metal tool, a digital level, a mini UV LED light, and a Breez Edge Pro smart thermostat, though these specific items are not named by the technician.

The video opens inside a well-lit Service Van Interior, where a technician, wearing a black smartwatch, sorts through a Cardboard Box filled with new Hilmar Tools. The technician's voice narrates, first mentioning a "thermostat for mini splits," then pulling out a blue and white box labeled 'FRESH AIR UV MINI UV LED'. He describes it as "the UV for mini splits," explaining that it "improves indoor quality" with its "UV blue light." The technician then states his intention to "stock up my van with these new tools." The camera pans to a Black Tool Bag, labeled 'TECH OT-LG' and 'VETO PRO PAC', which the technician identifies as his "install bag." He begins organizing various Hilmar Tools, including green and black screwdrivers, pliers, a Digital Level, and a Metal Tool (Hilmar), into the bag’s elastic loops and compartments. As he works, he mentions replacing an old tool with a "new hand seamer" and a "new zip tie tensioning tool." The environment is clean and organized, with white shelving units and red Milwaukee PACKOUT Toolboxes visible in the background. The technician's movements are deliberate as he neatly arranges the tools, adjusting their positions for optimal storage, accompanied by the sounds of tools being placed into the bag. He confirms these are "some of the Hilmar tools that we'll be adding to my install bag," and plans to "figure out what else we need to add" to make the "install bag complete," concluding by stating they will "make a list" for future additions.

The video opens with a close-up of a black 'VETO PRO PAC' tool bag, revealing an organized interior containing green-handled screwdrivers, a black flashlight, and a white container with red wires. A male voice states he has "blades and a charger in there" and will "throw this in here as well" as a hand adjusts the bag's contents. The scene transitions to the exterior of a white service van, where a red 'weather guard' metal tray is mounted on the side. The same male voice mentions he "installed this little thing" as a hand places two red '5X COIL CLEANER' spray cans into the tray, accompanied by metallic clanking sounds. He then adds that it's "perfect" for "cans like this." The camera then shifts to a pile of 'weather guard' cardboard boxes on a wooden pallet, where a hand removes a bright red plastic tray, showcasing its design and how it fits into the packaging, suggesting preparation for use in the sunny outdoor work environment. A different male voice concludes the segment, encouraging viewers to "like and subscribe" and follow on social media, adding, "Stay tuned, I'll have my van tour coming out very soon," referencing the white service van.
